exemplar.title: To what extent did the strategies used by Hutu extremists to eliminate perceived opposition influence the scale of the Rwandan Genocide from April 1994 to July 1994?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did the strategies used by Hutu extremists to eliminate perceived opposition influence the scale of the Rwandan Genocide from April 1994 to July 1994?

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and systematically, with clear temporal and conceptual limits.
Research question is explicit, focused and sustained throughout the essay.
Methodology is appropriately varied, drawing on relevant primary and secondary sources.
weaknesses: Occasional verbosity and minor overlap between sections (e.g., âIntroductionâ vs. âHistorical Contextâ).
Minor expression errors reduce precision in places.
A few sources of uncertain academic rigour are included without clear selection criteria.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student consistently demonstrates clear focus and a coherent research method. The topic and research question are both precise and aligned with the body of the work, and the methodology shows informed source selection. However, slight lapses in expression, section overlap, and uneven scholarly quality in a minority of sources prevent the work from achieving the full six marks in this criterion.

strengths: Source material is clearly relevant and integrated throughout the argument.
Demonstrates coherent and accurate subject knowledge, distinguishing key actors and chronology.
Uses appropriate historical terminology and concepts consistently.
weaknesses: In-text citations are occasionally incomplete or lack precise page references.
Some oversimplifications (e.g., direct causation claims) reduce analytical depth.
A few data points (e.g., sample size notation) are unclearly presented.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows secure knowledge and understanding, effectively applying relevant sources and employing accurate terminology. Critical handling of evidence could be deeperâcitations need tightening and certain claims require more nuanced supportâso the work merits the upper half of the band but falls short of a top mark.

strengths: Broad and relevant research base, integrating primary ICTR documents and key scholarly works.
Analysis links specific tactics to the research question with concrete examples.
Argument is logically structured with supported conclusions in most sections.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is uneven; some claims lack full demonstration or counter-evidence.
Evaluation of source bias and reliability is limited.
Discussion of alternative explanations and synthesis across factors is only partial.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Critical thinking is solidly placed in the lower end of the 7â9 band. The essay draws on a wide range of sources and makes largely supported analytical claims. However, inconsistent depth, limited critique of provenance, and only partial evaluation of competing perspectives prevent a higher holistic score.

strengths: All required structural elements are present (title page, contents, sections, references).
Headings and subheadings effectively guide the reader.
Page numbers are provided, aiding navigation.
weaknesses: Table of contents alignment is inconsistent, affecting readability.
Some sections overlap in function (e.g., âIntroductionâ vs. âHistorical Contextâ).
Missing figures/tables and verbose conclusion reduce overall polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets most IB requirements, with clear structure and generally consistent formatting. Minor lapses in alignment, missing graphics, and occasional over-verbosity keep this criterion from the top mark.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student includes a word count, title, and (in the original document) appears to number pages and use consistent font and heading hierarchy.  Tables and figures are captioned and referenced.  

Formatting is generally correct, but minor inconsistencies exist (e.g. occasional large blocks copied from web links, table of contents alignment, mixed citation styles).  These lapses keep the work from the very top of the band.  

Overall, most layout requirements are met and correctly applied, justifying a score of 3 in the 3-4 band.

A clear line of argument is presentâinternal Hutu strategies significantly shaped genocidal scaleâbut evaluation of alternative explanations (colonial legacy, external actors) is confined to brief remarks in the final pages.  The essay rarely interrogates source reliability or conflicting interpretations.  

Sections sometimes read as cumulative evidence rather than a fully synthesised debate, and weighing of the three strategic factors against each other is limited.  

Consequently the work meets the descriptor of a âreasoned argument with supported conclusionâ but offers only âpartialâ critical evaluation, placing it at the lower edge of the 4-6 bandâscore 4.

The student immediately states the topic, sets clear temporal limits (AprilâJuly 1994) and defines âscaleâ in relation to duration and violence.  The introduction and section headings are all directly aligned with the declared focus.  

There is no ambiguity about what is being investigatedâreaders can easily see that the essay will consider military operations, propaganda and political structure.  Minor lapses in expression (e.g. some wording errors) do not impede communication, and no irrelevant material is introduced.  

Because the topic is communicated accurately and the chosen frame is appropriate for systematic historical inquiry, the work merits the upper band; however, occasional verbosity and slight overlap between âHistorical Contextâ and âIntroductionâ prevent it from reaching the very top of the band, so 5/6 is awarded.

All required structural elementsâtitle page, abstract substitute within introduction, table of contents, sectioned body, conclusion and full reference listâare present and in logical order.  Headings and sub-headings guide the reader effectively.  

However, pagination is not shown in the excerpt and some sections (âHistorical Contextâ vs. âIntroductionâ) overlap, suggesting minor structural weakness.  

Hence the essay attains the appropriate-but-not-flawless level, meriting 3 within the 3-4 band.

The methodology is explicitly described: a mix of primary radio transcripts, UN documents, trial records, witness testimony and a range of secondary scholarship.  The reference list corroborates this breadth, and source types are appropriate to the question.  

Evidence of informed selection is visibleâe.g. Allison Des Forges and RomÃ©o Dallaire are recognised authorities, and the student purposefully chooses RTLM broadcasts for propaganda analysis.  Some sources are web links of uncertain academic rigour (e.g. âHutuGenocide.orgâ), indicating that selection is not flawless.  

Overall completeness and generally appropriate choices place the work solidly in the top band, though uneven scholarly quality of a minority of sources justifies 5 rather than 6.

The bibliography lists over twenty sources, including ICTR primary documents, UN reports, scholarly monographs and journal articles.  These are directly pertinent to the question and cover each analytical strand (military, propaganda, political structure).  

Within the narrative the student integrates many of these works rather than relying on a single piece of scholarship, indicating breadth and relevance.  However, provenance or bias is rarely interrogated, and some web sources of doubtful academic status slip in.  

Thus the research is âmostlyâ but not âconsistentlyâ appropriate in IB terms, fitting the 7-9 band at its lower endâscore 7.

The analysis moves beyond description by linking specific tactics (roadblocks, safe-house raids, RTLM rhetoric) to the overarching concept of scale, and it attempts to weigh their relative importance.  Many conclusions are supported with concrete examples and statistics (e.g. literacy rate, household tool ownership).  

Nonetheless, depth is uneven: some assertions (e.g. fear incentives or resource management) are asserted rather than fully demonstrated, and counter-arguments appear only briefly in the conclusion.  

Overall the essay delivers relevant, mostly supported analysis fitting the middle of the 4-6 bandâhence 5.

Primary and secondary material is woven into every sectionâdirect quotations from RTLM, Amnesty, ICTR indictments and Des Forges are applied to support specific claims about propaganda or command structures.  

The sources chosen are clearly relevant to the research question and are introduced with sufficient attribution.  Nonetheless, in-text referencing is occasionally incomplete (some quotations lack exact page/line citation) and evaluation of reliability is limited.  

Because relevance and appropriateness are strong but critical source handling is not consistently rigorous, the strand earns 5 within the 5-6 band.

The essay demonstrates secure knowledge of the chronology and actors of the genocide, accurately distinguishing the FAR, Interahamwe, CDR, RTLM, etc.  Explanations of how roadblocks functioned or why literacy rates mattered reveal an informed grasp of historical context.  

Understanding is coherent and mostly analytical rather than purely descriptive.  Some oversimplifications remain (e.g. equating land promises directly with participation without exploring counter-evidence), preventing a 6.  

Given clear, accurate exposition and generally effective use of evidence, a score of 5 is justified.

The research questionâ"To what extent did the strategies used by Hutu extremists to eliminate perceived opposition influence the scale of the Rwandan Genocide from April 1994 to July 1994?"âis explicit, focused and feasible.  It clearly identifies a cause-and-effect relationship within a well-defined time span.  

Throughout the body, each major section (military operations, propaganda, centralized power) links back to this question, demonstrating sustained focus.  Occasional digressions into colonial background are brief and serve contextualisation rather than derail the discussion.  

Given this clarity and sustained alignment, the strand sits firmly in the 5-6 band; a mid-band 5 is appropriate owing to a few moments where evaluative language (âpay[ed] the wayâ) is imprecise, slightly weakening sharpness.

The student employs appropriate historical terminologyâgenocide, militia, interim government, RTLM, RPF, Impuzamugambi, Civil Defence Programâcorrectly and consistently.  Concepts such as âstructural violenceâ and âdehumanisationâ are used accurately.  

Minor language errors appear (e.g. âpayed the wayâ, âstringent use of the wordâ), but they do not reflect conceptual misunderstanding.  

Consistent, accurate terminology throughout warrants a 5 in this top band.

annotations.comment: The propaganda section is clearly marked, helping the reader follow the analytical structure.
The title clearly states the investigationâs focus on the Rwandan Genocide, immediately orienting the reader.
The research question is explicit, well-focused, and defines variables clearly, aligning closely with the essayâs aim.
Word count is stated and within the 4 000-word limit, meeting formatting requirements.
The table of contents is comprehensive, but the alignment of entries and page numbers is inconsistent, affecting readability.
Economic motives are well noted, but the reliability of land-promise claims should be interrogated with counter-evidence.
The shift to the centralized power structure is well signposted, aiding reader navigation.
Page numbers are present on each page, aiding navigation through the document.
This contextual section is descriptive but could better link historical details to how they shaped the genocideâs scale relative to the research question.
The citation âRwanda:â lacks author details and date; ensure complete referencing for credibility.
The introduction begins clearly but ends mid-sentence, reducing clarity of the thesis statement.
The introduction provides context, but the thesis could be more concise and precise to sharpen the essayâs focus.
Minor spelling error: âpayedâ should be âpavedâ to refine language accuracy.
The methodology paragraph outlines varied source types but should explain criteria for source selection to strengthen methodological rigor.
The military operations heading is clear, but adding a linking sentence to show how these tactics impacted genocide scale would improve focus.
The description of militias is informative, yet deeper analysis of how this facilitated faster, larger-scale killings would enhance relevance to the research question.
The section on roadblocks explains the tactic but omits data quantifying its effect on kill rates; consider adding statistics or case studies.
The discussion of sexual violence is significant but requires precise citations and evaluation of source reliability.
The transition to safe-house raids is abrupt; adding a linking sentence about its impact on the genocideâs scale would improve coherence.
The safe-house massacre examples are vivid, yet integrating assessments of witness reliability or counter-accounts would deepen the analysis.
This paragraph introduces peacekeeper assassinations outside the stated scope; clarify whether external factors will be analyzed or adjust the research focus.
Effective use of RTLM quotes, but evaluate broadcaster bias and intent to critically assess source reliability.
Including the literacy rate is strong, but explicitly link this statistic to propaganda reach and resulting participation rates.
The section on Kangura magazine lacks specific excerpts or quotes; adding these would strengthen evidence for its mobilizing effect.
Insightful analysis of vague governmental language, but cite primary directives illustrating ambiguity to support the claim.
The table presents tool-ownership data clearly but requires analysis linking it to civilian participation in killings.
Caption should explicitly state the survey year and clarify the meaning of âN = 2.081â to avoid misinterpretation.
âN = 2.081 rural householdsâ is unclear in its unit and context; specify sample size and methodology.
Reference to Figure 1 lacks the actual graphic; include the visual to support the data interpretation.
Conclusion is clearly labelled, guiding the reader to the final synthesis.
The conclusion summarises internal factors well but introduces new topics; avoid fresh content and focus on synthesising earlier analysis.
The extended conclusion is verbose; consider condensing to emphasize core insights and maintain reader engagement.
References section is well titled, clearly marking the start of source documentation.
Several references are web-based with uncertain academic rigour; consider supplementing with peer-reviewed scholarship.
Reference formatting varies between styles; adopt one consistent format (APA, MLA) per IB guidelines.
Long URLs clutter the references; use DOIs or shortened links to improve readability and presentation.
